edges.evidence: DA1 physically associates with UBP15 in vitro and in vivo and modulates the stability of UBP15
DA1, DAR1 and DAR2 peptidases cleave TCP14, TCP15 and TCP22, leading to their inactivation and destabilization
DA1, DAR1 and DAR2 peptidases cleave TCP14, TCP15 and TCP22

edges.mechanism: peptidase cleavage of activating E3 ligase
edges.evidence: DA1 peptidase cleaves and destabilizes its activating E3 ligases (DA2 and BB) as part of negative feedback

edges.mechanism: peptidase cleavage of activating E3 ligase
edges.evidence: DA1 peptidase cleaves and destabilizes BIG BROTHER/EOD1 in a negative feedback loop

edges.mechanism: E3 ligase ubiquitylates and activates DA1 peptidase
edges.evidence: DA2 and BB E3 ligases ubiquitylate DA1 to activate its peptidase function

edges.mechanism: E3 ligase ubiquitylates and activates DA1 peptidase
edges.evidence: BB/EOD1 E3 ligase activates DA1 via monoubiquitylation
UBP12 and UBP13 negatively regulate the activity of the ubiquitin-dependent peptidases DA1, DAR1 and DAR2
UBP12 and UBP13 negatively regulate the activity of the ubiquitin-dependent peptidases DA1

edges.mechanism: restricts cell proliferation in maternal integuments
edges.evidence: DA1 acts maternally to affect seed growth by restricting cell proliferation in the integuments

edges.mechanism: RING E3 ligase restricts cell proliferation
edges.evidence: DA2 acts synergistically with DA1 to restrict cell proliferation in maternal integuments

edges.mechanism: RING E3 ligase restricts cell proliferation
edges.evidence: EOD1/BB and DA1 act synergistically to restrict cell proliferation
UBP15 influences seed size by promoting cell proliferation in the maternal integuments
SOD7/NGAL2 regulates seed size by limiting cell proliferation in the integument
DPA4 and SOD7 redundantly restrict seed size

edges.mechanism: maternal tissue contribution to seed volume
edges.evidence: maternal integument or seed coat sets upper limit to final seed size

edges.mechanism: non-cell-autonomous mobile growth signal from inner integument
edges.evidence: outer integuments were longer and consisted of more cells in pINO::KLU-expressing plants; klu-2 ovules had shorter integuments with fewer cells

edges.mechanism: cytochrome P450 generates mobile growth signal
edges.evidence: Seeds from klu-2 mutants were 13% lighter and 16% smaller than WT; KLU-overexpressing plants produced 11% heavier seeds

edges.mechanism: cytochrome P450 promotes integument cell expansion
edges.evidence: EOD3/CYP78A6 is primarily involved in regulation of cell expansion phases during later stages of integument development
CYP78A9 and EOD3/CYP78A6 have an overlapping function in the control of seed size; acting redundantly to regulate floral organ growth and integument development

edges.mechanism: WRKY transcription factor promoting cell expansion
edges.evidence: TTG2 gene acts maternally to promote cell expansion in integuments

edges.mechanism: restricts epidermal cell elongation in seed coat
edges.evidence: average length of seed coat epidermal cells in ap2 and wild-type seeds at 8 DAP were 41 and 30 μm respectively
ap2 endosperm cellularization initiated when embryos were at middle-heart stage at approximately 6 DAP, whereas wild-type cellularization began at 5 DAP

edges.mechanism: maternal sporophytic restriction of multiple seed compartments
edges.evidence: AP2 controls seed mass maternally, with ap2 mutants producing larger seeds than wild type
miR172 represses AP2 translation; known regulator of AP2 in floral and seed contexts
ARF2 negatively regulates auxin signaling and seed size via downregulation of cell division in the integument region
arf2 mutants produced 31% and 44% bigger seeds than wild-type (arf2-6 and arf2-7), suggesting ARF2 works as a negative regulator of seed growth
The peptidase DA1 cleaves and destabilizes WUSCHEL to control shoot apical meristem size
phosphorylation of MPK3/6 inactivate DA1, making it unstable
phosphorylation of MPK3/6 inactivate DA1, making it unstable

edges.mechanism: GA-GID1-DELLA complex triggers DELLA ubiquitylation and proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence: GA-GID1-DELLA complex enables binding with F-box proteins, DELLA protein polyubiquitylation and degradation by 26S proteasome
gid1a and gid1b seeds larger than wild-type...both GID1a/b expressed in ovules
gid1a and gid1b seeds larger than wild-type
GAI could trigger ANT induction during ovule development; ChIP-Seq identified putative GAI-binding sites upstream of ANT
CYCB1;1 expression level is increased in siliques at 3 DAP of gai-1
CYCB1;2 expression level is increased in gai-1 siliques

edges.mechanism: AP2-like TF promotes integument cell proliferation
edges.evidence: ANT activity would increase cell proliferation; overexpression causes oversized ovules/seeds
ANT provides key proliferative potential to the ovule primordia early in their development

edges.mechanism: cell cycle B-type cyclin drives mitosis
edges.evidence: B-type cyclins expressed shortly before and during mitosis...markers for mitosis and cell proliferation

edges.mechanism: MATERNAL EFFECT EMBRYO ARREST45 modulates maternal auxin biosynthesis
edges.evidence: MEE45 modulates maternal auxin biosynthesis and controls seed size by inducing AINTEGUMENTA
MEE45 controls seed size by inducing AINTEGUMENTA

perturbations.evidence_sentence: da1-1 mutant forms large seeds
Seeds produced by da2-1 were larger and heavier than wild-type seeds
overexpression of DA2 causes decreases in seed size and seed yield
da2-1 mutation synergistically enhanced seed size and weight phenotypes of da1-1
eod1 mutations synergistically enhanced seed size phenotype of da1-1
synergistic enhancement of seed size phenotype with da1-1 background
Seeds from ubp15-1 plants were significantly smaller and lighter than wild-type
gUBP15 transgenic plants formed larger and heavier seeds than wild-type
ubp15-1 is epistatic to da1-1 with respect to seed size and weight
Seeds from klu-2 mutants were 13% lighter and 16% smaller than seeds of WT plants
pINO::KLU transgene in a WT background caused a strong increase in seed size and weight (30% and 44%)
Loss of CYP78A9 function reduces seed size
double mutant seeds showed a purely additive phenotype
Seeds from klu-2 ttg2-1 double mutants were significantly smaller than either single mutant
ap2 mutants produce larger seeds than wild type
arf2 mutants produced 31% and 44% bigger seeds than wild-type plant
TTG2 gene acts maternally to promote cell expansion in integuments; mutant restricts integument elongation and causes precocious endosperm cellularization
ant mutants exhibit a reduction in the size of floral organs, abnormal integument development, and abnormal ovule development
gai-1 produces ~25% larger seeds and ~42% increased weight
rga-delta17 gain-of-function mutant produces larger seeds
rgl1-delta17 produces slightly larger seeds
rgl2-delta17 produces larger seeds
gaiT6 produces significantly smaller seeds
rgl1-1 produces smaller seeds
rgl2-1 produces smaller seeds
gid1a seeds larger than wild-type
gid1b seeds larger than wild-type

perturbations.condition: PBZ treatment (inhibits GA biosynthesis, stabilizes DELLA)
perturbations.evidence_sentence: Seeds of plants treated with PBZ were larger than those of any gain-of-function mutants
sod7 mutants produce larger seeds than wild-type due to loss of NGATHA-like repression
dpa4 loss-of-function increases seed size redundantly with sod7
sod7 dpa4 double mutants show strongly increased seed size
